summary: Analysis engine implements 4 modules: statistics, code-analysis, llm-judge, composite-scorer
context: FR-ANL-001 through FR-ANL-004 require automated code analysis, LLM-as-judge evaluation, statistical aggregation, and composite scoring. These form the analysis pipeline that processes raw benchmark results into scored, statistically validated comparisons.
rationale: Bottom-up dependency structure: statistics.ts is pure math with no deps on other analyzer modules, code-analysis.ts wraps simple-git/ts-morph/execa, llm-judge.ts wraps @anthropic-ai/sdk, composite-scorer.ts depends on statistics.ts and types. This keeps each module testable in isolation. Mann-Whitney U used for significance testing (non-parametric, doesn't assume normal distributions — appropriate for small benchmark run counts). Cohen's d for effect size. CES formula follows PRD Section 9.2 exactly.

alternatives.option: Use paired t-test instead of Mann-Whitney U
alternatives.reason_rejected: T-test assumes normal distribution; with small sample sizes (3-5 benchmark runs), non-parametric test is more appropriate. PRD mentions both as options.

alternatives.option: Use @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk for LLM judge instead of direct @anthropic-ai/sdk
alternatives.reason_rejected: The agent SDK is for orchestrating multi-turn sessions; single-turn evaluation calls are simpler with the direct SDK.
